Your scope is the SEALED-side families; two families are open-side assembly gaps handled outside the room (listed at the end).

  Public constants with mismatching capitalization

  (blank)
  entries like:  1. rule "Init":  name 'C', 'c'
In issue527 the same topic has two entries — "1. rule \"Four\":  name 'firSt', rule \"Two\":  name 'first'" and "2. rule \"One\":  name 'seconD', 'second'" — separated by a line containing exactly two spaces. Note the two entry shapes: when the mismatched spellings first occur in DIFFERENT rules each spelling carries its own rule attribution; same rule collapses to a name list. (2) ALIGNMENT: with >= 10 entries the reference right-aligns the index to the widest ("   1." three leading spaces … "  10." two), your render uses fixed two-space indent. Entries are separated by a line of exactly two spaces (you already do this). Probe multi-rule arity clashes to pin entry order and the alignment rule (right-align to widest index width — confirm at 9 vs 10 entries).

FAMILY 527-topics (issue527, 13 lines total) — the reference also contains two topics you omit entirely: "Fact capitalization issues" and "Fact arity issues" (each: underline, blank, numbered entries). Additionally your "Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization" section renders a "Possible reasons:" paragraph at a position the reference does not have one (duplicated preamble — the assembly layer outside the room also prepends this paragraph; make your body byte-match the target's BODY and record in round6/NOTES.md exactly which bytes you emit for this topic, so the assembly seam can be reconciled). Target: round6/targets/regression_trace_issue527.spthy.hs.txt is your ground truth for all of these.

OUT OF SCOPE (open-side assembly, fixed outside the room): (a) four SAPIC files each missing ONE blank line after the "Unbound variables"/"Special facts" topic underline; (b) Axioms_and_Induction missing the "Lemma annotations" topic HEADER (your body lines already match). If your probing contradicts this split — e.g. your checker fails to emit the lemma-annotation finding at all given a faithful input — say so in round6/NOTES.md.

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Materialize the Haskell reference wf-block for each of the 71 target theories
# into round5/targets/<safe>.hs.txt, using the sanctioned HS oracle only.
# Run this from inside the sealed workspace; it touches only the HS binary and
# the corpus .spthy inputs (both permitted materials).
set -u
here=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
CORPUS="${CORPUS:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples}"
FLAGS_MAP="${FLAGS_MAP:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/file_flags.tsv}"
ORACLE="$here/../oracle/wf_oracle.sh"
mkdir -p "$here/targets"
wf_block() {
  awk '
    /^\/\* All wellformedness checks were successful\. \*\/$/ { print; next }
    /^WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!$/  { f=1 }
    f { print }
    f && /^\*\/$/ { f=0 }'
}
while read -r REL; do
  f="$CORPUS/$REL"
  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING $REL" >&2; continue; }
  fl=$(awk -F'\t' -v r="$REL" '!/^#/ && $1==r {print $2}' "$FLAGS_MAP")
  fl=${fl//@cd/}
  safe=$(printf '%s' "$REL" | tr '/' '_')
  # wf_oracle.sh runs the HS binary (no --prove needed — wf prints at load).
  "$ORACLE" "$f" $fl 2>/dev/null | wf_block > "$here/targets/$safe.hs.txt"
done < "$here/wf_gate_diffs.txt"
echo "wrote $(ls "$here/targets" | wc -l) HS reference blocks to targets/"

16	/// Topics that follow the public-names report in the canonical order; the
17	/// public-names report is inserted immediately before the first of these that
18	/// is present in the report. (Unbound variables and Fresh public constants
19	/// precede the insertion point and are therefore NOT anchors.)
20	pub fn after_public_names_topics() -> Vec<&'static str> {
21	    vec![
22	        checks::T_SORTS,
23	        checks::T_RESERVED,
24	        checks::T_RESERVED_PREFIX,
25	        checks::T_FR,
26	        checks::T_SPECIAL,
27	        checks::T_FACT_CAP,
28	        checks::T_ARITY,
29	        checks::T_MULT,
30	        checks::T_LHSRHS,
31	        checks::T_LEFT,
32	        checks::T_RIGHT,
33	        checks::T_QUANT_SORTS,
34	        checks::T_FORMULA_TERMS,
35	        checks::T_GUARD,
36	        checks::T_LEMMA_ANNOT,
37	        checks::T_MULRESTRICT,
38	        checks::T_NAT,
39	        checks::T_SUBTERM,
40	    ]
41	}
42	
43	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order. The "Formula terms" check runs
44	/// in free-variable-only mode; for reducible-function coverage use
45	/// [`check_theory_with_reducible`].
46	pub fn check_theory(thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
47	    check_theory_with_reducible(thy, &std::collections::BTreeSet::new())
48	}
49	
50	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order, passing a caller-computed set
51	/// of reducible function-symbol names to the "Formula terms" check (theories
52	/// with `equations:` make some user functions reducible; the caller derives
53	/// that set - typically from the equation theory / Maude - and this pipeline
54	/// only consumes it).
55	pub fn check_theory_with_reducible(
56	    thy: &Theory,
57	    reducible: &std::collections::BTreeSet<String>,
58	) -> WfReport {
59	    let mut report: WfReport = Vec::new();
60	    // Base pipeline (public names inserted afterwards at its anchor point).
61	    report.extend(checks::unbound_variables(thy)); // 1
62	    report.extend(checks::fresh_public_constants(thy)); // 2
63	    report.extend(checks::mismatching_sorts(thy)); // 4
64	    report.extend(checks::reserved_names(thy)); // 5
65	    report.extend(checks::reserved_prefixes(thy)); // 6 (diff mode only)
66	    report.extend(checks::fr_facts(thy)); // 7
67	    report.extend(checks::special_facts(thy)); // 8
68	    report.extend(checks::fact_capitalization(thy)); // 8b (precedes arity)
69	    report.extend(checks::fact_arity(thy)); // 9
70	    report.extend(checks::fact_multiplicity(thy)); // 10
71	    report.extend(checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs(thy)); // 11
72	    report.extend(checks::diff_left_right(thy)); // 12, 13 (diff mode only)
73	    // The Quantifier-sorts / Formula-terms / Formula-guardedness bundle is
74	    // emitted per formula item (lemmas then restrictions), interleaved by topic.
75	    report.extend(checks::formula_reports(thy, reducible)); // 14, 15, 16
76	    report.extend(checks::lemma_annotations(thy)); // 17
77	    report.extend(checks::multiplication_restriction(thy)); // 17
78	    report.extend(checks::nat_sorts(thy)); // 18
79	    report.extend(checks::subterm_convergence(thy)); // 19
80	
81	    let pn = checks::public_names_report(thy); // 3
82	    let anchors = after_public_names_topics();
83	    insert_wf_before(&mut report, pn, &anchors);
84	    report
85	}

90	/// the message text is a documented gap (see BEHAVIOR.md).
91	pub fn check_if_lemmas_in_theory(lemma_names: &[String], thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
92	    let present = checks::theory_lemma_names(thy);
93	    let mut out = Vec::new();

98	                format!("lemma `{}' referenced but not present in theory", name),

101	    }

212	#[test]
213	fn guard_equality_acceptances() {
214	    // Reference-accepted equality-guard shapes -> empty report.
215	    let node_i = || var("i", SortHint::Node);
216	    let cases: Vec<Formula> = vec![
217	        // g5_e_eqbare: Ex z. z = 'c'
218	        exists(vec![var("z", SortHint::Untagged)], eq(mv("z"), pl("c"))),
219	        // g5_e_eqinner: Ex z. h(z) = 'c'
220	        exists(vec![var("z", SortHint::Untagged)], eq(app("h", vec![mv("z")]), pl("c"))),
221	        // g5_e_eqpair: All k #i. A(k)@#i ==> Ex y r. k = <y, r>
222	        forall(
223	            vec![var("k", SortHint::Untagged), node_i()],
224	            imp(
225	                act(fact("A", vec![mv("k")]), Term::Var(node_i())),
226	                exists(
227	                    vec![var("y", SortHint::Untagged), var("r", SortHint::Untagged)],
228	                    eq(mv("k"), pair(vec![mv("y"), mv("r")])),
229	                ),
230	            ),
231	        ),
232	        // g5_e_eqmset: All x y #i. B(x,y)@#i ==> Ex z. (x++z) = y
233	        forall(
234	            vec![var("x", SortHint::Untagged), var("y", SortHint::Untagged), node_i()],
235	            imp(
236	                act(fact("B", vec![mv("x"), mv("y")]), Term::Var(node_i())),
237	                exists(
238	                    vec![var("z", SortHint::Untagged)],
239	                    eq(Term::BinOp(BinOp::Union, Box::new(mv("x")), Box::new(mv("z"))), mv("y")),
240	                ),
241	            ),
242	        ),
243	        // g5_e_eqchain: Ex z w. (z = 'c') & (w = h(z))
244	        exists(
245	            vec![var("z", SortHint::Untagged), var("w", SortHint::Untagged)],
246	            conj(eq(mv("z"), pl("c")), eq(mv("w"), app("h", vec![mv("z")]))),
247	        ),
248	        // g5_e_actorder: All k #j. C(k)@#j ==> Ex y w #i. (w = h(y)) & A(y)@#i
249	        forall(
250	            vec![var("k", SortHint::Untagged), var("j", SortHint::Node)],
251	            imp(
252	                act(fact("C", vec![mv("k")]), Term::Var(var("j", SortHint::Node))),
253	                exists(
254	                    vec![var("y", SortHint::Untagged), var("w", SortHint::Untagged), node_i()],
255	                    conj(
256	                        eq(mv("w"), app("h", vec![mv("y")])),
257	                        act(fact("A", vec![mv("y")]), Term::Var(node_i())),
258	                    ),
259	                ),
260	            ),
261	        ),
262	        // g5_e_pairboth: Ex y z. <y, z> = 'c'
263	        exists(
264	            vec![var("y", SortHint::Untagged), var("z", SortHint::Untagged)],
265	            eq(pair(vec![mv("y"), mv("z")]), pl("c")),
266	        ),
267	        // g5_e_selfnoop: Ex z. (z = 'c') & (z = z)
268	        exists(
269	            vec![var("z", SortHint::Untagged)],
270	            conj(eq(mv("z"), pl("c")), eq(mv("z"), mv("z"))),
271	        ),
272	        // g5_e_eqtemp: All #j x. A(x)@#j ==> Ex #i. #i = #j
273	        forall(
274	            vec![var("j", SortHint::Node), var("x", SortHint::Untagged)],
275	            imp(
276	                act(fact("A", vec![mv("x")]), Term::Var(var("j", SortHint::Node))),
277	                exists(
278	                    vec![node_i()],
279	                    eq(Term::Var(node_i()), Term::Var(var("j", SortHint::Node))),
280	                ),
281	            ),
282	        ),
283	        // g5_e_nest: Ex x. Ex #i. A(x)@#i  (existential fusion)
284	        exists(
285	            vec![var("x", SortHint::Untagged)],
286	            exists(vec![node_i()], act(fact("A", vec![mv("x")]), Term::Var(node_i()))),
287	        ),
288	        // g5_a_nest: All x. All #i. A(x)@#i ==> C(x)@#i  (forall fusion)
289	        forall(
290	            vec![var("x", SortHint::Untagged)],
291	            forall(
292	                vec![node_i()],
293	                imp(
294	                    act(fact("A", vec![mv("x")]), Term::Var(node_i())),
295	                    act(fact("C", vec![mv("x")]), Term::Var(node_i())),
296	                ),
297	            ),
298	        ),
299	        // g5_a_eqg2: All w x #i. ((w = h(x)) & A(x)@#i) ==> C(w)@#i
300	        forall(
301	            vec![var("w", SortHint::Untagged), var("x", SortHint::Untagged), node_i()],
302	            imp(
303	                conj(
304	                    eq(mv("w"), app("h", vec![mv("x")])),
305	                    act(fact("A", vec![mv("x")]), Term::Var(node_i())),
306	                ),
307	                act(fact("C", vec![mv("w")]), Term::Var(node_i())),
308	            ),
309	        ),
310	        // g5_a_eqonly: All w. (w = 'c') ==> (w = 'c')
311	        forall(
312	            vec![var("w", SortHint::Untagged)],
313	            imp(eq(mv("w"), pl("c")), eq(mv("w"), pl("c"))),
314	        ),
315	    ];
316	    for (i, f) in cases.into_iter().enumerate() {
317	        let thy = theory("T", vec![probe_rule(), lemma_all("L", f)]);
318	        let r = check_theory(&thy);
319	        assert!(r.is_empty(), "case {} reported: {}", i, render_report(&r));
320	    }
321	}

656	/// Render one topic block (header + underline + body) the way the report does.

| file | family | disposition |
|---|---|---|
| esorics23-bluetooth/models/ble.spthy | listfmt/factusage | **FIXED sealed-side** |

## 1. FIXED sealed-side — ble listfmt/factusage

`checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs` previously deduplicated the LHS-only list by
fact identity `(name, arity, multiplicity)`, collapsing a fact reused across
several rules into a single entry. The reference emits **one entry per premise
occurrence** with **no deduplication at all**, in **pure source order**:

- probe `lhs1`: `AA`@A, `BB`@A, `CC`@B, `AA`@C — the two `AA` entries are NOT
  grouped/adjacent → source order, not grouped by fact name.
- probe `lhs2`: `DD` appearing twice in one rule → two entries; `EE` arity 1 and
  arity 2 → two entries.
- probe `lhs3`: a fact identity present on ANY RHS suppresses EVERY one of its
  LHS occurrences (identity-level, per-occurrence exclusion).

Fix: the `seen` dedup vector was removed; the RHS-identity exclusion and the
right-aligned numbering ("   1." … "  10.", entries separated by a two-space
line) are unchanged. ble now yields the expected 10 entries.

Regression fixtures/tests (`tests/round6_tests.rs`,
`tests/fixtures/t6_ble_lhs.txt`):
`ble_lhs_not_rhs_per_rule_entries`, `lhs_not_rhs_no_dedup_source_order`,
`lhs_not_rhs_rhs_identity_suppresses_all_occurrences`.

## 2. Pinned (sealed already correct) — CentralizedMonitor pubcap

`checks::public_names_report` already produces the reference bytes. Probe `pc1`
(single translated rule "Init" using public constants 'c' and 'C') reproduces
the CentralizedMonitor target exactly:

```
Public constants with mismatching capitalization$
================================================$
$
Identifiers are case-sensitive, mismatched capitalizations are considered as different, i.e., 'ID' is different from 'id'. Check the capitalization of your identifiers.$
$
  1. rule "Init":  name 'C', 'c'$
```

`tests/fixtures/t6_centralizedmonitor_pubcap.txt`. The 7-line gate divergence
was the open side lacking this whole topic; the sealed checker emits it. The
open side must integrate the sealed `public_names_report` output. (The
`Accountability (RP check)` block above it in the full target is emitted by the
accountability subsystem, not the wf checker, and is not a sealed concern.)

## 3. Pinned + SEAM — issue527

The sealed report is byte-identical to the round6 target through the last
sealed topic. Verified by diffing the target (truncated before the Maude
`Message Derivation Checks` block) against `tests/fixtures/t5_issue527.txt`:
the ONLY delta is a single blank line at target line 110 — the join separator
the open side inserts before the out-of-scope `Message Derivation Checks`
block. So pubcap, fact-capitalization, fact-arity and the mismatching-sorts
body are all already correct sealed-side.

opening bytes (`WfError.message` for topic

1. Identifiers are case sensitive, i.e.,'x' and 'X' are considered to be different.$

**Seam instruction for the open side:** the sealed body ALREADY contains the

gate observed. The full topic body for this section is produced entirely by the
